_meta.description: Programmatic SEO dataset for ResumeAI-Bot. Each array drives one page template. Slugs are URL-safe. Content fields are fed into the page templates to produce unique 600-900 word pages. Edit freely.
_meta.valueProp: AI builds your resume and auto-applies to jobs in 50+ countries

countries.name: Germany
countries.resumeNotes: German CVs (Lebenslauf) are factual and structured; photo and date of birth still common but optional and declining. 1-2 pages. A short cover letter (Anschreiben) is often expected.
countries.visa: EU Blue Card for skilled workers above the salary threshold; the Opportunity Card (Chancenkarte) lets qualified people enter to job-hunt.
countries.language: Many tech/engineering roles are English-first; German strongly helps elsewhere.

countries.name: the Netherlands
countries.resumeNotes: Dutch CVs are concise (max 2 pages), no photo needed, focus on results. Cover letter (motivatiebrief) common.
countries.visa: Highly Skilled Migrant permit (sponsored employer) and the orientation year (zoekjaar) visa for recent graduates.
countries.language: English is widely accepted in tech and international companies.

countries.name: the United Kingdom
countries.resumeNotes: UK CVs are 2 pages, no photo, no date of birth. Strong personal statement at top.
countries.visa: Skilled Worker visa requires a licensed sponsor; Global Talent and High Potential Individual routes exist.
countries.language: English only.

countries.name: Ireland
countries.resumeNotes: 2-page CV, no photo. Highlight EU work rights status clearly.
countries.visa: Critical Skills Employment Permit for in-demand roles; General Employment Permit otherwise.
countries.language: English only.

countries.name: Canada
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page resume, no photo, no personal details. Tailor to each posting; quantify achievements.
countries.visa: Express Entry points system rewards skills, English/French, and experience; Provincial Nominee Programs add routes.
countries.language: English (French in Quebec).

countries.name: the United States
countries.resumeNotes: 1 page (early career) to 2 pages, no photo, no personal data. Heavy keyword/ATS optimization.
countries.visa: H-1B (lottery), O-1 for extraordinary ability, L-1 intra-company transfers. Sponsorship is competitive.
countries.language: English only.

countries.name: Australia
countries.resumeNotes: Resume can run 2-4 pages; include a skills summary. No photo. Address selection criteria where listed.
countries.visa: Skilled Independent (subclass 189) and Skilled Nominated (190) points-based; Employer Sponsored (482/186).
countries.language: English only.

countries.name: New Zealand
countries.resumeNotes: 2-3 page CV, no photo. Mention visa/work status up front.
countries.visa: Skilled Migrant Category (points-based); Accredited Employer Work Visa.
countries.language: English only.

countries.name: the United Arab Emirates
countries.resumeNotes: Photo and nationality are commonly included in the Gulf. Keep it to 2 pages. Hiring is fast and volume-driven.
countries.visa: Employer-sponsored work visa is standard; the Green Visa and Golden Visa offer longer-term options.
countries.language: English is the business language.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.resumeNotes: Include photo and personal details (Gulf norm). 2 pages. Highlight relevant regional experience.
countries.visa: Employer-sponsored work visa (Iqama); Premium Residency for some.
countries.language: English common in multinationals; Arabic helps.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page resume; including a photo and nationality is common in Asia. Quantify results.
countries.visa: Employment Pass for professionals above the salary threshold; Tech.Pass for tech leaders.
countries.language: English is the working language.

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.resumeNotes: CVs often include a photo and date of birth. 2-3 pages. Note language skills (DE/FR/IT/EN).
countries.visa: Permits are quota-limited for non-EU nationals; EU/EFTA citizens have easier access.
countries.language: German, French, Italian, plus strong English in multinationals.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.resumeNotes: Concise 1-2 page CV, no photo needed. Personal cover letter valued.
countries.visa: Work permit requires a job offer; EU Blue Card available.
countries.language: English is widely spoken and accepted in tech.

countries.name: Norway
countries.resumeNotes: 2-page CV, no photo necessary. References valued.
countries.visa: Skilled Worker permit with a job offer; job-seeker visa for skilled professionals.
countries.language: English common in tech and energy.

countries.name: Denmark
countries.resumeNotes: Concise CV with a personal cover letter. No photo required.
countries.visa: Pay Limit Scheme and Positive List fast-track in-demand roles.
countries.language: English widely accepted in international firms.

countries.name: France
countries.resumeNotes: French CV is 1 page, photo optional. Lettre de motivation often expected.
countries.visa: Talent Passport (Passeport Talent) for skilled workers and tech.
countries.language: French strongly preferred; English roles exist in tech/startups.

countries.name: Spain
countries.resumeNotes: CV can include a photo (common). 1-2 pages.
countries.visa: Highly Qualified Professional visa; the Digital Nomad visa for remote workers.
countries.language: Spanish preferred; English roles in tech and tourism.

countries.name: Portugal
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV, photo common. Highlight language skills.
countries.visa: Tech Visa and the D8 Digital Nomad visa make Portugal popular with remote workers.
countries.language: Portuguese preferred; English common in tech and tourism.

countries.name: Poland
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV. NoFluffJobs is the go-to for transparent IT salaries.
countries.visa: Work permit (Type A) with a job offer; EU Blue Card.
countries.language: Polish for most roles; English strong in IT and BPO.

countries.name: the Czech Republic
countries.resumeNotes: Concise CV, photo optional. English CVs accepted in IT.
countries.visa: Employee Card combines work and residence permit.
countries.language: Czech for most; English strong in IT/shared services.

countries.name: Italy
countries.resumeNotes: European-format CV, photo common. Note EU work rights.
countries.visa: EU Blue Card; Decreto Flussi quotas for some roles.
countries.language: Italian preferred; English roles in multinationals.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.resumeNotes: Concise CV; note language abilities (NL/FR/EN).
countries.visa: Single Permit for work + residence; EU Blue Card.
countries.language: Dutch and French; English in EU bodies and multinationals.

countries.name: Austria
countries.resumeNotes: German-style CV with photo common. 1-2 pages.
countries.visa: Red-White-Red Card (points-based) for skilled workers.
countries.language: German preferred; English in tech/tourism.

countries.name: Finland
countries.resumeNotes: Concise CV, no photo necessary. English CVs fine in tech.
countries.visa: Specialist residence permit; fast-track for certain roles.
countries.language: English widely spoken, especially in tech and gaming.

countries.name: Japan
countries.resumeNotes: Japanese employers may request a rirekisho (standard form) plus a Western CV. Photo common.
countries.visa: Engineer/Specialist in Humanities visa; Highly Skilled Professional points system.
countries.language: Japanese expected for most; English roles in global tech.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.resumeNotes: Resume with photo common. English-teaching and tech roles open to foreigners.
countries.visa: E-7 (specially designated activities) for professionals; E-2 for English teachers.
countries.language: Korean preferred; English in education and global tech.

countries.name: Hong Kong
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV, photo optional. Finance roles dominate.
countries.visa: General Employment Policy; Top Talent Pass Scheme.
countries.language: English is a working language in business.

countries.name: India
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page resume; Naukri is dominant. Strong keyword optimization.
countries.visa: Employment Visa for foreign professionals (salary threshold applies).
countries.language: English is widely used in business and IT.

countries.name: China
countries.resumeNotes: Bilingual (EN/CN) resume helps. Photo common.
countries.visa: Z visa for work; foreigner work permit classified A/B/C.
countries.language: Mandarin preferred; English in education and multinationals.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV, photo common. Portuguese resume preferred.
countries.visa: Temporary work visa (VITEM) sponsored by employer.
countries.language: Portuguese preferred; English in tech/multinationals.

countries.name: Mexico
countries.resumeNotes: CV with photo common. Spanish resume preferred.
countries.visa: Temporary Resident visa with work permission.
countries.language: Spanish preferred; English in tech and nearshoring.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV, photo common. Spanish resume preferred.
countries.visa: Work visa sponsored by employer; growing remote-work options.
countries.language: Spanish preferred; English in tech.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.resumeNotes: 2-3 page CV. Note work eligibility clearly.
countries.visa: Critical Skills Work Visa for in-demand occupations.
countries.language: English is widely used in business.

countries.name: Israel
countries.resumeNotes: 1-2 page CV. Strong tech sector; English CVs accepted.
countries.visa: B-1 work visa sponsored by employer.
countries.language: Hebrew preferred; English strong in tech.

countries.name: Turkey
countries.resumeNotes: CV with photo common. Turkish resume preferred.
countries.visa: Work permit applied for by employer.
countries.language: Turkish preferred; English in multinationals.

countries.name: Qatar
countries.resumeNotes: Gulf format with photo and personal details. 2 pages.
countries.visa: Employer-sponsored work visa standard.
countries.language: English is the business language.

countries.name: Luxembourg
countries.resumeNotes: Multilingual CV valued (EN/FR/DE). Concise.
countries.visa: EU Blue Card; salaried worker permit.
countries.language: French, German, English all used in business.

countries.name: Estonia
countries.resumeNotes: Concise English CV fine in tech. Digital-first hiring.
countries.visa: Startup Visa and Digital Nomad Visa pioneered here.
countries.language: English widely accepted in the startup scene.

countries.name: Remote (Global Companies)
countries.resumeNotes: Lead with timezone overlap and async-work strengths. Quantify remote impact. ATS-optimized.
countries.visa: No visa needed when hired as a contractor or through an Employer of Record (EOR).
countries.language: English is standard for global remote roles.

jobBoards.name: LinkedIn
jobBoards.note: The largest global professional network. Auto-apply via Easy Apply listings; great for international and remote roles.

jobBoards.name: Indeed
jobBoards.note: The world's biggest job aggregator across nearly every country.

jobBoards.name: Adzuna
jobBoards.note: Aggregates millions of listings across 20+ countries. Included in the free plan.

jobBoards.name: RemoteOK
jobBoards.note: A top board for remote-first roles at global companies. Included in the free plan.

jobBoards.name: Glassdoor
jobBoards.note: Listings plus company reviews and salary data.

jobBoards.name: StepStone
jobBoards.note: A leading board across Germany and parts of Europe.

jobBoards.name: SEEK
jobBoards.note: The dominant board in Australia and New Zealand.

jobBoards.name: We Work Remotely
jobBoards.note: One of the largest remote-work communities.

jobBoards.name: Bayt
jobBoards.note: The leading job site across the Middle East and Gulf.

jobBoards.name: JobStreet
jobBoards.note: A major board across Southeast Asia.

professions.name: Software Engineer
professions.note: Lead with stack, scale, and shipped impact. ATS keyword matching matters most here.

professions.name: Data Analyst
professions.note: Quantify the decisions your analysis drove, not just the tools.

professions.name: Data Scientist
professions.note: Show models in production and business outcomes.

professions.name: Product Manager
professions.note: Frame achievements as outcomes (growth, retention), not features shipped.

professions.name: Nurse
professions.note: List licenses/registrations clearly; many countries require credential recognition.

professions.name: Doctor / Physician
professions.note: Highlight registration status and language certification for the target country.

professions.name: Accountant
professions.note: Note certifications (ACCA, CPA) and systems experience.

professions.name: Marketing Manager
professions.note: Lead with measurable results (CAC, ROAS, pipeline).

professions.name: Sales Representative
professions.note: Numbers win: quota attainment, deal size, growth.

professions.name: UX Designer
professions.note: Link a portfolio; show before/after impact.

professions.name: Project Manager
professions.note: Show on-time, on-budget delivery and certifications (PMP).

professions.name: DevOps Engineer
professions.note: Quantify reliability and deployment-speed gains.

professions.name: Mechanical Engineer
professions.note: List standards and certifications relevant to the target country.

professions.name: Civil Engineer
professions.note: Highlight licensure and major projects.

professions.name: Teacher
professions.note: Note teaching license and language proficiency; ESL is a common entry route abroad.

professions.name: Graphic Designer
professions.note: Always link a portfolio.

professions.name: Customer Support Specialist
professions.note: Language skills and CSAT/response metrics stand out, especially for remote roles.

professions.name: Financial Analyst
professions.note: Show models built and decisions influenced.

professions.name: HR Manager
professions.note: Show scale (headcount, hires) and program outcomes.

professions.name: Business Analyst
professions.note: Bridge tech and business; quantify process improvements.

competitors.name: Sonara
competitors.summary: Sonara.ai was a funded auto-apply startup that scanned listings and submitted AI-tailored applications. It has shut down, leaving its users without a tool.
whySwitch: It has shut down — users need a replacement now
It was US/LinkedIn-centric, not built for international applicants
Users criticized it as an opaque 'black box' with inconsistent results
ourEdge: Covers 50+ countries, not just US LinkedIn
Real free tier (3 apps/day) and a 30-day money-back guarantee

competitors.name: LazyApply
competitors.summary: LazyApply is a Chrome extension that auto-applies at high volume across LinkedIn, Indeed, and ZipRecruiter.
whySwitch: High volume can mean low-relevance, spammy applications
No real free tier; lifetime pricing is a bigger upfront commitment
ourEdge: 50+ countries including the whole EU, Gulf, and Asia
AI tailors the resume per role to lift relevance
Free tier to try before paying

competitors.name: LoopCV
competitors.summary: LoopCV scans job boards, filters by your criteria, and auto-applies or queues roles, with email outreach and tracking.
whySwitch: Capable but the setup and filtering can be complex
Resume tailoring is limited compared with a per-role AI rewrite
ourEdge: Per-role AI resume generation built in
Comparable price ($19.99/mo) with a money-back guarantee

competitors.name: Jobright
competitors.summary: Jobright is an AI job-search copilot with matching, resume tailoring, and 'insider connection' suggestions, focused on the US market.
whySwitch: US-focused; weaker for international and relocation job seekers
Full auto-apply agent has been limited/beta
ourEdge: Built for international and relocation searches across 50+ countries
Lower entry price with a 30-day guarantee

competitors.name: AIApply
competitors.summary: AIApply automates applications and offers cover letters, interview prep, and a job board.
ourEdge: Predictable monthly/annual pricing instead of weekly
50+ country coverage and per-role AI resumes
